compiled_state.title: dso:onboarding skill: multi-part question in single turn -> violates one-question-per-message contract
compiled_state.author: Test
compiled_state.description:
## Incident Overview
/dso:onboarding asks multiple questions combined into a single message rather than asking one question per turn, violating the one-question-per-message dialogue contract.

## Example (Observed)
> Next question: Where does this app run (or where do you plan to deploy it)? For example, is it hosted somewhere like Vercel, AWS, or government cloud? And does it connect to any external services — like a job postings API, a database, or an AI service for analyzing listings?

This single message combines:
1. Deployment location question
2. External services / integrations question

## Expected vs Observed
- **Expected**: /dso:onboarding asks exactly one question per turn, waits for the user's answer, then asks the next question.
- **Observed**: /dso:onboarding bundles multiple distinct questions (deployment environment + external services) into a single message.

## Impact
Users are forced to answer multiple questions at once, increasing cognitive load and making it harder to give precise answers. This also makes the onboarding conversation harder to follow and reduces the quality of information gathered.

## Affected Component
plugins/dso/skills/onboarding/SKILL.md (or equivalent) — question sequencing / dialogue flow logic

## Notes
Reported by user during interactive session. The fix should enforce a strict one-question-per-turn discipline throughout the entire onboarding dialogue.
